## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Next: phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-credentialed (₢BBAAK) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Sibling pace to BBAAB and BBAAJ. Implements the more complex half of pristine-lifecycle: retriever lifecycle (case 4) and director lifecycle (case 5). Each case creates a throwaway governor + the role SA, exercises the credential via access-probe, then forfeits.
  
  Includes the assay/canonical file movement choreography: SA-creator writes RBRA to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`; test stages to `assay/`; test copies `assay/` → canonical role path for probe; test cleans canonical and assay before forfeit. Mirrors operator-flow file movements rather than extending the probe to accept arbitrary paths.
  
  **Absorbs rbrr-completion fix-up.** BBAAJ landed cases 2-3 with `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` setting only `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` after levy. `zrbrr_enforce` (which runs on every non-`rbgp_depot_levy` invocation per `rbgp_cli.sh` differential furnish) requires `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` and `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` non-blank too — both blanked by marshal-zero. Credentialed cases 2-5 will fail at the first non-levy command without those fields populated. K lands two new `rbgp_fact_*` files for the values `rbgp_depot_levy` computes (`z_repository_name`, `z_pool_stem`), generalizes the install helper to write all three post-levy fields in one commit, and back-patches cases 2 and 3 to use it. Cases 4 and 5 follow the same pattern.
  
  **BBAAJ landed at `e59c49b8`** — `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` and the throwaway-prefix consts are stable and `pub(crate)`. The thread-local invocation context is bridged by `rbtdrc_with_ctx` (now `pub(crate)` after BBAAJ); cases 4-5 use it the same way cases 2-3 do.
  
  ## Locked patterns
  
  Implementation patterns are locked in the paddock under "Implementation patterns (locked during BBAAJ)". Read those before implementing. Reuse:
  
  - `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(root)` — landed `pub(crate)` at e59c49b8; call as first step of cases 4 and 5 (idempotent — no-op if already invoked in same fixture run).
  - `RBTDRP_THROWAWAY_CLOUD_PREFIX` / `RBTDRP_THROWAWAY_RUNTIME_PREFIX` — `pub(crate)` constants; reference by name rather than re-declaring `"prlc-"` / `"prlr-"` string literals.
  - Field-const pattern — BBAAJ extracted `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_*` as private module-scope consts; mirror this pattern for any new RBRR field-name strings K introduces (see `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` below).
  - Fact-file invocation pattern (BBAAJ-locked): tabtargets that emit identifying values write `rbgp_fact_*` files via `buf_write_fact`; cases consume via `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`.
  - `rbtdrc_with_ctx` (now `pub(crate)` after BBAAJ) for thread-local context bridging — same shape as case 3.
  - `rbtdri_find_tabtarget` (with role as imprint) for `rbtd-ap` — imprint channel.
  - Git shell-out style: `rbtdrp_git_add_and_commit` (BBAAJ-factored helper) handles the `git add <file> && git commit -m <msg>` pair.
  
  ## Work
  
  ### Step 1 — rbrr-post-levy completion (foundational, lands first)
  
  **Bash + spec edits (do BEFORE Rust changes):**
  
  - `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh`: add two consts inside the existing readonly block (next to the `RBGP_FACT_*` pair from BBAAJ):
    ```
    readonly RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY="rbgp_fact_gar_repository"
    readonly RBGP_FACT_GCB_POOL_STEM="rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem"
    ```
  - `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh::rbgp_depot_levy`: after `z_repository_name` is computed (search for the local declaration), `buf_write_fact "${RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY}" "${z_repository_name}"`. Same for `z_pool_stem` (search the body — assigned during pool-stem derivation around the worker-pool block). Place each `buf_write_fact` call immediately after the value becomes known so a partial-failure run still emits whatever was computed.
  - `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`: extend the Payor Fact Files section (added in BBAAJ) with two new linked terms — `rbgp_fact_gar_repository` and `rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem`. Add their attribute references to the mapping section near the existing `rbgp_fact_*` entries. Apply CMK MCM linked-term shape (attribute reference + anchor + definition with `axpof_fact` voicing).
  
  **Rust edits:**
  
  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrp_pristine.rs`:
    - Add `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` / `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` private consts (mirror the existing `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`).
    - Add `RBTDRP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY` / `RBTDRP_FACT_GCB_POOL_STEM` consts mirroring the bash side.
    - **Rename** `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id(root, project_id)` → `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr(root, project_id, gar_repository, gcb_pool_stem)`. Single rbrr.env read → `rbtdrp_replace_env_fields` with three entries → single write → single commit. Commit message: `"pristine-lifecycle fixture: set RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID/RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY/RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM"`.
    - Patch `rbtdrp_depot_lifecycle_impl` (case 2): after the levy invocation, read all three facts; call `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr` BEFORE the `list-present` invocation (which runs enforce). Tee the three values to per-case files for diagnostics.
    - Patch `rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle_impl` (case 3): replace the existing `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` call with the new three-arg helper, sourcing all three values from the levy result's facts.
  
  ### Step 2 — Cases 4 and 5
  
  #### Pre-conditions (per case)
  
  - Each case pre-checks canonical role path empty (refuse with diagnostic if not — single-case independence requires clean canonical paths for the role being tested AND for governor, since each case mantles its own throwaway governor).
  
  #### Case 4: retriever-lifecycle (one GCP project per run + light read probe)
  
  - Call `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(root)` (idempotent).
  - Pre-check: governor and retriever canonical RBRA paths empty.
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dL", &["pristr01"], &[])` — levy own throwaway depot (distinct moniker from cases 2/3).
  - Read all three post-levy facts (`rbgp_fact_depot_project_id`, `rbgp_fact_gar_repository`, `rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem`).
  - Call `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr(root, project_id, gar_repository, gcb_pool_stem)`.
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aM", &[], &[])` → mantle throwaway governor; capture governor SA email via `rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email` fact.
  - Move: `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/$instance.rbra` → `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` → `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` (canonical).
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aC", &[], &[])` → charter throwaway retriever (its RBRA in `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`).
  - Move: `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/$instance.rbra` → `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` (overwrite) → `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` (canonical).
  - `rbtdri_invoke_imprint(ctx, "rbtd-ap", "retriever", &[])` → access-probe proves retriever permissions resolve.
  - Cleanup: rm canonical retriever, rm canonical governor, rm assay.
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aF", &[&retriever_email], &[])` → forfeit retriever.
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aF", &[&governor_email], &[])` → forfeit governor.
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dU", &[&project_id], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")])` → unmake own depot.
  
  #### Case 5: director-lifecycle (one GCP project per run + light Cloud Build / GAR write probe)
  
  Same shape as case 4 but with `rbw-aK` (knight director) and `rbtd-ap director`. Moniker: `pristd01`.
  
  #### Open question for implementation time (carried from prior docket revs)
  
  `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE` are bash-side derived constants from `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`. The Rust fixture either re-derives the paths from RBRR fields or queries them by sourcing rbdc — pick at implementation time. Re-derivation is simpler if the path shape is stable (e.g., `${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/<role>/rbra.env` plus assay equivalent).
  
  Similarly: cases 4/5 need to capture the role-SA email + RBRA file location after charter/knight. Whether `rbgg_charter_retriever` / `rbgg_knight_director` already emit fact files, or need new ones (`rbgp_fact_retriever_sa_email` / `rbgp_fact_director_sa_email`), or whether the email is discoverable via the BURD_OUTPUT_DIR file naming — choose at implementation time. Prefer fact files for symmetry with the governor pattern (BBAAJ).
  
  ### Step 3 — Section + manifest registration
  
  - Add section `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` to `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` (after `pristine-lifecycle-simple` from BBAAJ). Final layout: §1 gate → §2 simple → §3 credentialed.
  - Add colophon consts to `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`: `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_CHARTER` (rbw-aC), `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_KNIGHT` (rbw-aK).
  - `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ACCESS_PROBE` (rbtd-ap) is already in `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE` — re-declare for `pristine-lifecycle` for clarity.
  - Extend the `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` required-colophon array (currently 6 entries from BBAAJ) with the three new entries.
  
  ## Verification
  
  - Build green: `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh`.
  - Unit tests green: `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh`. The renamed helper invalidates the BBAAJ-landed `rbtdtp_pristine.rs` test surface; if any test references `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` by name, update or replace.
  - Fast suite green: `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`.
  - Single-case `depot-lifecycle` (post-rename): list/unmake commands now succeed past `zrbrr_enforce` because all three RBRR fields are populated. Operator-action.
  - Single-case `governor-lifecycle`: same, plus governor lifecycle. Operator-action.
  - Single-case `retriever-lifecycle`: creates throwaway depot+gov+ret, probe passes, cleans up cleanly; refuses with diagnostic if canonical role paths occupied. Operator-action.
  - Single-case `director-lifecycle`: same for director. Operator-action.
  - Full fixture on marshal-zero state: cases 1-5 all pass with one rbrr.env install commit (prefixes), then four post-levy commits (one per case 2/3/4/5), each documenting the depot they're running against.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Cases 1-3 design (BBAAB and BBAAJ); cases 2/3 are only patched here to consume the new helper, not redesigned.
  - Canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC).
  - Crash-recovery cleanup of orphan SAs and orphan throwaway depots — handled via soft-delete graveyard discipline (depot) and operator-driven `rbw-aF` (SAs); a partial-failure run accumulates a soft-delete-pending project, which is acceptable per the "start-over-from-zero" failure-mode contract.
  
  ## Quota footprint (full fixture run)
  
  Four throwaway depots per pristine-lifecycle run (one per case 2, 3, 4, 5). Each goes to `DELETE_REQUESTED` on case cleanup; soft-delete graveyard accepted. Plus one canonical depot from BBAAC for the rest of `rbw-tP`. Five quota-bearing project creations per pristine release-qualification cycle.

  - `rbtdri_find_tabtarget` (with role as imprint) for `rbtd-ap` — imprint channel.
  - Git shell-out style: `rbtdrp_git_add_and_commit` (BBAAJ-factored helper) handles the `git add <file> && git commit -m <msg>` pair.
  
  ## Work
  
  ### Step 1 — rbrr-post-levy completion (foundational, lands first)
  
  **Bash + spec edits (do BEFORE Rust changes):**
  
  - `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh`: add two consts inside the existing readonly block (next to the `RBGP_FACT_*` pair from BBAAJ):
    ```
    readonly RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY="rbgp_fact_gar_repository"
    readonly RBGP_FACT_GCB_POOL_STEM="rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem"
    ```
  - `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh::rbgp_depot_levy`: after `z_repository_name` is computed (search for the local declaration), `buf_write_fact "${RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY}" "${z_repository_name}"`. Same for `z_pool_stem` (search the body — assigned during pool-stem derivation around the worker-pool block). Place each `buf_write_fact` call immediately after the value becomes known so a partial-failure run still emits whatever was computed.
  - `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`: extend the Payor Fact Files section (added in BBAAJ) with two new linked terms — `rbgp_fact_gar_repository` and `rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem`. Add their attribute references to the mapping section near the existing `rbgp_fact_*` entries. Apply CMK MCM linked-term shape (attribute reference + anchor + definition with `axpof_fact` voicing).
  
  **Rust edits:**
  
  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrp_pristine.rs`:
    - Add `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` / `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` private consts (mirror the existing `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`).
    - Add `RBTDRP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY` / `RBTDRP_FACT_GCB_POOL_STEM` consts mirroring the bash side.
    - **Rename** `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id(root, project_id)` → `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr(root, project_id, gar_repository, gcb_pool_stem)`. Single rbrr.env read → `rbtdrp_replace_env_fields` with three entries → single write → single commit. Commit message: `"pristine-lifecycle fixture: set RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID/RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY/RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM"`.
    - Patch `rbtdrp_depot_lifecycle_impl` (case 2): after the levy invocation, read all three facts; call `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr` BEFORE the `list-present` invocation (which runs enforce). Tee the three values to per-case files for diagnostics.
    - Patch `rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle_impl` (case 3): replace the existing `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` call with the new three-arg helper, sourcing all three values from the levy result's facts.
  
  ### Step 2 — Cases 4 and 5
  
  #### Pre-conditions (per case)
  
  - Each case pre-checks canonical role path empty (refuse with diagnostic if not — single-case independence requires clean canonical paths for the role being tested AND for governor, since each case mantles its own throwaway governor).
  
  #### Case 4: retriever-lifecycle (one GCP project per run + light read probe)
  
  - Call `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(root)` (idempotent).
  - Pre-check: governor and retriever canonical RBRA paths empty.
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dL", &["pristr01"], &[])` — levy own throwaway depot (distinct moniker from cases 2/3).
  - Read all three post-levy facts (`rbgp_fact_depot_project_id`, `rbgp_fact_gar_repository`, `rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem`).
  - Call `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr(root, project_id, gar_repository, gcb_pool_stem)`.
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aM", &[], &[])` → mantle throwaway governor; capture governor SA email via `rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email` fact.
  - Move: `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/$instance.rbra` → `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` → `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` (canonical).
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aC", &[], &[])` → charter throwaway retriever (its RBRA in `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`).
  - Move: `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/$instance.rbra` → `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` (overwrite) → `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` (canonical).
  - `rbtdri_invoke_imprint(ctx, "rbtd-ap", "retriever", &[])` → access-probe proves retriever permissions resolve.
  - Cleanup: rm canonical retriever, rm canonical governor, rm assay.
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aF", &[&retriever_email], &[])` → forfeit retriever.
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aF", &[&governor_email], &[])` → forfeit governor.
  - `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dU", &[&project_id], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")])` → unmake own depot.
  
  #### Case 5: director-lifecycle (one GCP project per run + light Cloud Build / GAR write probe)
  
  Same shape as case 4 but with `rbw-aK` (knight director) and `rbtd-ap director`. Moniker: `pristd01`.
  
  #### Open question for implementation time (carried from prior docket revs)
  
  `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE` are bash-side derived constants from `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`. The Rust fixture either re-derives the paths from RBRR fields or queries them by sourcing rbdc — pick at implementation time. Re-derivation is simpler if the path shape is stable (e.g., `${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/<role>/rbra.env` plus assay equivalent).
  
  Similarly: cases 4/5 need to capture the role-SA email + RBRA file location after charter/knight. Whether `rbgg_charter_retriever` / `rbgg_knight_director` already emit fact files, or need new ones (`rbgp_fact_retriever_sa_email` / `rbgp_fact_director_sa_email`), or whether the email is discoverable via the BURD_OUTPUT_DIR file naming — choose at implementation time. Prefer fact files for symmetry with the governor pattern (BBAAJ).
  
  ### Step 3 — Section + manifest registration
  
  - Add section `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` to `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` (after `pristine-lifecycle-simple` from BBAAJ). Final layout: §1 gate → §2 simple → §3 credentialed.
  - Add colophon consts to `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`: `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_CHARTER` (rbw-aC), `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_KNIGHT` (rbw-aK).
  - `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ACCESS_PROBE` (rbtd-ap) is already in `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE` — re-declare for `pristine-lifecycle` for clarity.
  - Extend the `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` required-colophon array (currently 6 entries from BBAAJ) with the three new entries.
  
  ## Verification
  
  - Build green: `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh`.
  - Unit tests green: `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh`. The renamed helper invalidates the BBAAJ-landed `rbtdtp_pristine.rs` test surface; if any test references `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` by name, update or replace.
  - Fast suite green: `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`.
  - Single-case `depot-lifecycle` (post-rename): list/unmake commands now succeed past `zrbrr_enforce` because all three RBRR fields are populated. Operator-action.
  - Single-case `governor-lifecycle`: same, plus governor lifecycle. Operator-action.
  - Single-case `retriever-lifecycle`: creates throwaway depot+gov+ret, probe passes, cleans up cleanly; refuses with diagnostic if canonical role paths occupied. Operator-action.
  - Single-case `director-lifecycle`: same for director. Operator-action.
  - Full fixture on marshal-zero state: cases 1-5 all pass with one rbrr.env install commit (prefixes), then four post-levy commits (one per case 2/3/4/5), each documenting the depot they're running against.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Cases 1-3 design (BBAAB and BBAAJ); cases 2/3 are only patched here to consume the new helper, not redesigned.
  - Canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC).
  - Crash-recovery cleanup of orphan SAs and orphan throwaway depots — handled via soft-delete graveyard discipline (depot) and operator-driven `rbw-aF` (SAs); a partial-failure run accumulates a soft-delete-pending project, which is acceptable per the "start-over-from-zero" failure-mode contract.
  
  ## Quota footprint (full fixture run)
  
  Four throwaway depots per pristine-lifecycle run (one per case 2, 3, 4, 5). Each goes to `DELETE_REQUESTED` on case cleanup; soft-delete graveyard accepted. Plus one canonical depot from BBAAC for the rest of `rbw-tP`. Five quota-bearing project creations per pristine release-qualification cycle.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Design decisions
10	
11	### Single operator prerequisite chain
12	
13	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
14	
15	### Entry contract — load-bearing
16	
17	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
18	- Working tree clean
19	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
20	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
21	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
22	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
23	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
24	
25	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
26	
27	### Pristine-lifecycle fixture architecture
28	
29	The marshal-zero gate AND the SA/depot lifecycle exercises live as cases inside one new theurge fixture (`pristine-lifecycle`):
30	
31	| # | Case | Idempotent? | Cost |
32	|---|------|-------------|------|
33	| 1 | `marshal-zero-attestation` | N/A — gate | Free |
34	| 2 | `depot-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway-named) | One GCP project per run (soft-delete graveyard accepted) |
35	| 3 | `governor-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway-named) | One GCP project per run (case levies its own throwaway depot to satisfy `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` precondition; depot is unmade in case cleanup) |
36	| 4 | `retriever-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway governor + retriever) | Free + light read probe |
37	| 5 | `director-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway governor + director) | Light Cloud Build / GAR write probe |
38	
39	Cases 4 and 5 each create their own throwaway governor (single-case independence is load-bearing); after the case, both throwaway SAs are forfeited. Cases 2-5 are idempotent against existing canonical state — they can run while a canonical depot/SA chain exists, against any state.
40	
41	**Single-case run** (e.g., `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.pristine-lifecycle.sh depot-lifecycle`) skips case 1's gate and exercises just the named case. **Full-fixture run** goes through case 1 first; case 1 gates the rest.
42	
43	### Canonical infrastructure setup is a separate phase
44	
45	After the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes, `rbw-tP` proceeds to canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC): mantle real governor + deploy real RBRA, charter real retriever + deploy RBRA, knight real director + deploy RBRA, levy canonical depot. This persistent state is what reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs depend on.
46	
47	Two depots created per pristine run: one throwaway (case 2 of fixture) + one canonical (BBAAC). Both quota-bearing. Case 3 also levies+unmakes its own throwaway depot — counted as a third quota-bearing event but cleaned up within the case.
48	
49	### Failure mode contract
50	
51	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
52	
53	### Implementation patterns (locked during BBAAJ)
54	
55	Durable choices binding all pristine-lifecycle case implementations (BBAAB, BBAAJ, BBAAK, and future BBAAC vessel-commit cases).
56	
57	**Throwaway-prefix mechanism: edit `.rbk/rbrr.env` + git commit.** Cases 2-5 need non-blank `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX`/`RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX` to invoke depot/SA tabtargets. The fixture mimics the human workflow: open rbrr.env, edit, `git add` + `git commit`. After the fixture runs, HEAD has walked off marshal-zero — recovery is `rbw-MZ`, matching the "start-over-from-zero" failure mode above.
58	
59	Rejected alternative — env-var override: `rbrr_cli.sh` kindle does `source rbrr_regime.sh; source ${RBBC_rbrr_file}; zrbrr_kindle; zrbrr_enforce` — sourcing rbrr.env overwrites any pre-set env, then enforce locks `readonly`. Pre-set env is silently discarded. Confirmed by tracing.
60	
61	Rejected alternative — in-memory restore (RAII): mutates rbrr.env without committing, leaves a transient dirty-tree window where a crash or sibling tooling sees uncommitted state that isn't operator-authored. Edit-and-commit is honest.
62	
63	**Idempotent install helper.** A shared function `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` reads current rbrr.env values; if blank (post-marshal-zero), writes throwaway values and commits; if already throwaway, no-op. Cases 2-5 call this as their first step. One commit per fixture run, not per case.
64	
65	**Depot precondition for governor-mantle (case 3).** `rbgp_governor_mantle` requires `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` set — post-marshal-zero it is blank. Case 3 levies its own throwaway depot (named distinctly from case 2's, e.g. `pristg01` vs `pristq01`), edits rbrr.env to set `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and commits, runs the governor lifecycle, then unmakes the depot in cleanup. The same pattern (commit a small targeted edit, run, clean up) is the model for future cases that need additional rbrr/rbrv/rbrn fields populated.
66	
67	**Tabtarget invocation style — fact files over stdout parsing.** Tabtargets that emit identifying values (depot project_ids, SA emails) write `rbgp_fact_*` files via `buf_write_fact`; cases consume via `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`. The BURV-isolated invoke also makes invoke counters and per-call output capture available to the case. Handbook-style tabtargets without facts (e.g., `rbtdrf_handbook.rs` cases) keep the raw `Command::new(&tt).current_dir(&root)` style. For imprint-channel tabtargets like `rbtd-ap` (cases 4-5), use `rbtdri_find_tabtarget` with the role as imprint.
68	
69	**Section structure inside `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`.**
70	- §1 `pristine-lifecycle-gate` — case 1 only (BBAAB-landed)
71	- §2 `pristine-lifecycle-simple` — cases 2-3 (BBAAJ)
72	- §3 `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` — cases 4-5 (BBAAK)
73	
74	`rbtdrc_fixture_fail_fast` is true for pristine-lifecycle, so §1 failure short-circuits §2 and §3 in full-fixture mode. Single-case mode skips the gate.
75	
76	**Vessel commits beyond BBAAJ/BBAAK.** As pristine-tier extends to BBAAC and beyond (reliquary inscribe / enshrine / ordain / kludge), additional commits in vessel `rbrv.env` files and similar regime artifacts use the same edit-and-commit shape. The qualification's commit trail is the operator's audit trail.
77	
78	**Git shell-out style.** Mirror jjx/vvx — `Command::new("git").args([...]).current_dir(&root).output()`. Stage with explicit file path (never `git add -A`); commit messages produced via Rust string formatting. No `--no-verify`, no `--amend`.
79	
80	### Tier layering
81	
82	Three tiers, escalating cost:
83	
84	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
85	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
86	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
87	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
88	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
89	
90	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
91	
92	### Operator scope
93	
94	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section (5 human steps).
95	
96	### Coupling to ₣A_
97	
98	This heat runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces. ₣A_'s AAE+AAF+AAG sequence is the one-time cutover that informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence; pristine-tier construction proceeds in parallel and codifies the lessons. BBAAG's first end-to-end run is the natural validation point for both heats together.
99	
100	## References
101	
102	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap; AAE+AAF+AAG one-time burn-in informs but doesn't codify
103	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — RBLM Lifecycle Marshal; `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime, deletes all RBRAs, blanks hallmark/depot-scoped vessel fields. Marshal-zero signature is baked here per BBAAB.
104	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
105	- `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhocd_credential_director.sh` / `rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh` — handbook tracks describing director "build access" and retriever "pull/tally access" — operations the lifecycle cases 4 and 5 must verify
106	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
107	- Recent spook commits informing the bug class: kludge-aware-charge-prereq (₢BAAAH), ZRBOB_PROJECT extraction (f7146d1d), rbob_charged_predicate fix (a8eb7311), reliquary integrity-broken negative-test residue (recent ₣BA verification)
108	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_handbook.rs` — non-context tabtarget shell-out reference pattern for handbook-style cases (no fact files)
109	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdri_invocation.rs` — fact-file invocation pattern: `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact` for cases that need to capture identifying values
110	- `Tools/rbk/rbrr_cli.sh` — kindle architecture documented in Implementation patterns; sourcing-over-env behavior locked the throwaway mechanism choice
111	
112	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAK
113	
114	## Character
115	
116	Sibling pace to BBAAB and BBAAJ. Implements the more complex half of pristine-lifecycle: retriever lifecycle (case 4) and director lifecycle (case 5). Each case creates a throwaway governor + the role SA, exercises the credential via access-probe, then forfeits.
117	
118	Includes the assay/canonical file movement choreography: SA-creator writes RBRA to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`; test stages to `assay/`; test copies `assay/` → canonical role path for probe; test cleans canonical and assay before forfeit. Mirrors operator-flow file movements rather than extending the probe to accept arbitrary paths.
119	
120	**Absorbs rbrr-completion fix-up.** BBAAJ landed cases 2-3 with `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` setting only `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` after levy. `zrbrr_enforce` (which runs on every non-`rbgp_depot_levy` invocation per `rbgp_cli.sh` differential furnish) requires `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` and `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` non-blank too — both blanked by marshal-zero. Credentialed cases 2-5 will fail at the first non-levy command without those fields populated. K lands two new `rbgp_fact_*` files for the values `rbgp_depot_levy` computes (`z_repository_name`, `z_pool_stem`), generalizes the install helper to write all three post-levy fields in one commit, and back-patches cases 2 and 3 to use it. Cases 4 and 5 follow the same pattern.
121	
122	**BBAAJ landed at `e59c49b8`** — `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` and the throwaway-prefix consts are stable and `pub(crate)`. The thread-local invocation context is bridged by `rbtdrc_with_ctx` (now `pub(crate)` after BBAAJ); cases 4-5 use it the same way cases 2-3 do.
123	
124	## Locked patterns
125	
126	Implementation patterns are locked in the paddock under "Implementation patterns (locked during BBAAJ)". Read those before implementing. Reuse:
127	
128	- `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(root)` — landed `pub(crate)` at e59c49b8; call as first step of cases 4 and 5 (idempotent — no-op if already invoked in same fixture run).
129	- `RBTDRP_THROWAWAY_CLOUD_PREFIX` / `RBTDRP_THROWAWAY_RUNTIME_PREFIX` — `pub(crate)` constants; reference by name rather than re-declaring `"prlc-"` / `"prlr-"` string literals.
130	- Field-const pattern — BBAAJ extracted `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_*` as private module-scope consts; mirror this pattern for any new RBRR field-name strings K introduces (see `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` below).
131	- Fact-file invocation pattern (BBAAJ-locked): tabtargets that emit identifying values write `rbgp_fact_*` files via `buf_write_fact`; cases consume via `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`.
132	- `rbtdrc_with_ctx` (now `pub(crate)` after BBAAJ) for thread-local context bridging — same shape as case 3.
133	- `rbtdri_find_tabtarget` (with role as imprint) for `rbtd-ap` — imprint channel.
134	- Git shell-out style: `rbtdrp_git_add_and_commit` (BBAAJ-factored helper) handles the `git add <file> && git commit -m <msg>` pair.
135	
136	## Work
137	
138	### Step 1 — rbrr-post-levy completion (foundational, lands first)
139	
140	**Bash + spec edits (do BEFORE Rust changes):**
141	
142	- `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh`: add two consts inside the existing readonly block (next to the `RBGP_FACT_*` pair from BBAAJ):
143	  ```
144	  readonly RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY="rbgp_fact_gar_repository"
145	  readonly RBGP_FACT_GCB_POOL_STEM="rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem"
146	  ```
147	- `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh::rbgp_depot_levy`: after `z_repository_name` is computed (search for the local declaration), `buf_write_fact "${RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY}" "${z_repository_name}"`. Same for `z_pool_stem` (search the body — assigned during pool-stem derivation around the worker-pool block). Place each `buf_write_fact` call immediately after the value becomes known so a partial-failure run still emits whatever was computed.
148	- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`: extend the Payor Fact Files section (added in BBAAJ) with two new linked terms — `rbgp_fact_gar_repository` and `rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem`. Add their attribute references to the mapping section near the existing `rbgp_fact_*` entries. Apply CMK MCM linked-term shape (attribute reference + anchor + definition with `axpof_fact` voicing).
149	
150	**Rust edits:**
151	
152	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrp_pristine.rs`:
153	  - Add `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` / `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` private consts (mirror the existing `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`).
154	  - Add `RBTDRP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY` / `RBTDRP_FACT_GCB_POOL_STEM` consts mirroring the bash side.
155	  - **Rename** `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id(root, project_id)` → `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr(root, project_id, gar_repository, gcb_pool_stem)`. Single rbrr.env read → `rbtdrp_replace_env_fields` with three entries → single write → single commit. Commit message: `"pristine-lifecycle fixture: set RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID/RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY/RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM"`.
156	  - Patch `rbtdrp_depot_lifecycle_impl` (case 2): after the levy invocation, read all three facts; call `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr` BEFORE the `list-present` invocation (which runs enforce). Tee the three values to per-case files for diagnostics.
157	  - Patch `rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle_impl` (case 3): replace the existing `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` call with the new three-arg helper, sourcing all three values from the levy result's facts.
158	
159	### Step 2 — Cases 4 and 5
160	
161	#### Pre-conditions (per case)
162	
163	- Each case pre-checks canonical role path empty (refuse with diagnostic if not — single-case independence requires clean canonical paths for the role being tested AND for governor, since each case mantles its own throwaway governor).
164	
165	#### Case 4: retriever-lifecycle (one GCP project per run + light read probe)
166	
167	- Call `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(root)` (idempotent).
168	- Pre-check: governor and retriever canonical RBRA paths empty.
169	- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dL", &["pristr01"], &[])` — levy own throwaway depot (distinct moniker from cases 2/3).
170	- Read all three post-levy facts (`rbgp_fact_depot_project_id`, `rbgp_fact_gar_repository`, `rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem`).
171	- Call `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr(root, project_id, gar_repository, gcb_pool_stem)`.
172	- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aM", &[], &[])` → mantle throwaway governor; capture governor SA email via `rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email` fact.
173	- Move: `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/$instance.rbra` → `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` → `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` (canonical).
174	- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aC", &[], &[])` → charter throwaway retriever (its RBRA in `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`).
175	- Move: `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/$instance.rbra` → `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` (overwrite) → `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` (canonical).
176	- `rbtdri_invoke_imprint(ctx, "rbtd-ap", "retriever", &[])` → access-probe proves retriever permissions resolve.
177	- Cleanup: rm canonical retriever, rm canonical governor, rm assay.
178	- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aF", &[&retriever_email], &[])` → forfeit retriever.
179	- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aF", &[&governor_email], &[])` → forfeit governor.
180	- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dU", &[&project_id], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")])` → unmake own depot.
181	
182	#### Case 5: director-lifecycle (one GCP project per run + light Cloud Build / GAR write probe)
183	
184	Same shape as case 4 but with `rbw-aK` (knight director) and `rbtd-ap director`. Moniker: `pristd01`.
185	
186	#### Open question for implementation time (carried from prior docket revs)
187	
188	`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE` are bash-side derived constants from `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`. The Rust fixture either re-derives the paths from RBRR fields or queries them by sourcing rbdc — pick at implementation time. Re-derivation is simpler if the path shape is stable (e.g., `${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/<role>/rbra.env` plus assay equivalent).
189	
190	Similarly: cases 4/5 need to capture the role-SA email + RBRA file location after charter/knight. Whether `rbgg_charter_retriever` / `rbgg_knight_director` already emit fact files, or need new ones (`rbgp_fact_retriever_sa_email` / `rbgp_fact_director_sa_email`), or whether the email is discoverable via the BURD_OUTPUT_DIR file naming — choose at implementation time. Prefer fact files for symmetry with the governor pattern (BBAAJ).
191	
192	### Step 3 — Section + manifest registration
193	
194	- Add section `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` to `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` (after `pristine-lifecycle-simple` from BBAAJ). Final layout: §1 gate → §2 simple → §3 credentialed.
195	- Add colophon consts to `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`: `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_CHARTER` (rbw-aC), `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_KNIGHT` (rbw-aK).
196	- `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ACCESS_PROBE` (rbtd-ap) is already in `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE` — re-declare for `pristine-lifecycle` for clarity.
197	- Extend the `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` required-colophon array (currently 6 entries from BBAAJ) with the three new entries.
198	
199	## Verification
200	
201	- Build green: `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh`.
202	- Unit tests green: `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh`. The renamed helper invalidates the BBAAJ-landed `rbtdtp_pristine.rs` test surface; if any test references `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` by name, update or replace.
203	- Fast suite green: `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`.
204	- Single-case `depot-lifecycle` (post-rename): list/unmake commands now succeed past `zrbrr_enforce` because all three RBRR fields are populated. Operator-action.
205	- Single-case `governor-lifecycle`: same, plus governor lifecycle. Operator-action.
206	- Single-case `retriever-lifecycle`: creates throwaway depot+gov+ret, probe passes, cleans up cleanly; refuses with diagnostic if canonical role paths occupied. Operator-action.
207	- Single-case `director-lifecycle`: same for director. Operator-action.
208	- Full fixture on marshal-zero state: cases 1-5 all pass with one rbrr.env install commit (prefixes), then four post-levy commits (one per case 2/3/4/5), each documenting the depot they're running against.
209	
210	## Out of scope
211	
212	- Cases 1-3 design (BBAAB and BBAAJ); cases 2/3 are only patched here to consume the new helper, not redesigned.
213	- Canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC).
214	- Crash-recovery cleanup of orphan SAs and orphan throwaway depots — handled via soft-delete graveyard discipline (depot) and operator-driven `rbw-aF` (SAs); a partial-failure run accumulates a soft-delete-pending project, which is acceptable per the "start-over-from-zero" failure-mode contract.
215	
216	## Quota footprint (full fixture run)
217	
218	Four throwaway depots per pristine-lifecycle run (one per case 2, 3, 4, 5). Each goes to `DELETE_REQUESTED` on case cleanup; soft-delete graveyard accepted. Plus one canonical depot from BBAAC for the rest of `rbw-tP`. Five quota-bearing project creations per pristine release-qualification cycle.

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Design decisions

### Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

### Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

### Pristine-lifecycle fixture architecture

The marshal-zero gate AND the SA/depot lifecycle exercises live as cases inside one new theurge fixture (`pristine-lifecycle`):

| # | Case | Idempotent? | Cost |
|---|------|-------------|------|
| 1 | `marshal-zero-attestation` | N/A — gate | Free |
| 2 | `depot-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway-named) | One GCP project per run (soft-delete graveyard accepted) |
| 3 | `governor-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway-named) | One GCP project per run (case levies its own throwaway depot to satisfy `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` precondition; depot is unmade in case cleanup) |
| 4 | `retriever-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway governor + retriever) | Free + light read probe |
| 5 | `director-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway governor + director) | Light Cloud Build / GAR write probe |

Cases 4 and 5 each create their own throwaway governor (single-case independence is load-bearing); after the case, both throwaway SAs are forfeited. Cases 2-5 are idempotent against existing canonical state — they can run while a canonical depot/SA chain exists, against any state.

**Single-case run** (e.g., `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.pristine-lifecycle.sh depot-lifecycle`) skips case 1's gate and exercises just the named case. **Full-fixture run** goes through case 1 first; case 1 gates the rest.

### Canonical infrastructure setup is a separate phase

After the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes, `rbw-tP` proceeds to canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC): mantle real governor + deploy real RBRA, charter real retriever + deploy RBRA, knight real director + deploy RBRA, levy canonical depot. This persistent state is what reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs depend on.

Two depots created per pristine run: one throwaway (case 2 of fixture) + one canonical (BBAAC). Both quota-bearing. Case 3 also levies+unmakes its own throwaway depot — counted as a third quota-bearing event but cleaned up within the case.

### Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

### Implementation patterns (locked during BBAAJ)

Durable choices binding all pristine-lifecycle case implementations (BBAAB, BBAAJ, BBAAK, and future BBAAC vessel-commit cases).

**Throwaway-prefix mechanism: edit `.rbk/rbrr.env` + git commit.** Cases 2-5 need non-blank `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX`/`RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX` to invoke depot/SA tabtargets. The fixture mimics the human workflow: open rbrr.env, edit, `git add` + `git commit`. After the fixture runs, HEAD has walked off marshal-zero — recovery is `rbw-MZ`, matching the "start-over-from-zero" failure mode above.

Rejected alternative — env-var override: `rbrr_cli.sh` kindle does `source rbrr_regime.sh; source ${RBBC_rbrr_file}; zrbrr_kindle; zrbrr_enforce` — sourcing rbrr.env overwrites any pre-set env, then enforce locks `readonly`. Pre-set env is silently discarded. Confirmed by tracing.

Rejected alternative — in-memory restore (RAII): mutates rbrr.env without committing, leaves a transient dirty-tree window where a crash or sibling tooling sees uncommitted state that isn't operator-authored. Edit-and-commit is honest.

**Idempotent install helper.** A shared function `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` reads current rbrr.env values; if blank (post-marshal-zero), writes throwaway values and commits; if already throwaway, no-op. Cases 2-5 call this as their first step. One commit per fixture run, not per case.

**Depot precondition for governor-mantle (case 3).** `rbgp_governor_mantle` requires `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` set — post-marshal-zero it is blank. Case 3 levies its own throwaway depot (named distinctly from case 2's, e.g. `pristg01` vs `pristq01`), edits rbrr.env to set `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and commits, runs the governor lifecycle, then unmakes the depot in cleanup. The same pattern (commit a small targeted edit, run, clean up) is the model for future cases that need additional rbrr/rbrv/rbrn fields populated.

**Tabtarget invocation style — fact files over stdout parsing.** Tabtargets that emit identifying values (depot project_ids, SA emails) write `rbgp_fact_*` files via `buf_write_fact`; cases consume via `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`. The BURV-isolated invoke also makes invoke counters and per-call output capture available to the case. Handbook-style tabtargets without facts (e.g., `rbtdrf_handbook.rs` cases) keep the raw `Command::new(&tt).current_dir(&root)` style. For imprint-channel tabtargets like `rbtd-ap` (cases 4-5), use `rbtdri_find_tabtarget` with the role as imprint.

**Section structure inside `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`.**
- §1 `pristine-lifecycle-gate` — case 1 only (BBAAB-landed)
- §2 `pristine-lifecycle-simple` — cases 2-3 (BBAAJ)
- §3 `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` — cases 4-5 (BBAAK)

`rbtdrc_fixture_fail_fast` is true for pristine-lifecycle, so §1 failure short-circuits §2 and §3 in full-fixture mode. Single-case mode skips the gate.

**Vessel commits beyond BBAAJ/BBAAK.** As pristine-tier extends to BBAAC and beyond (reliquary inscribe / enshrine / ordain / kludge), additional commits in vessel `rbrv.env` files and similar regime artifacts use the same edit-and-commit shape. The qualification's commit trail is the operator's audit trail.

**Git shell-out style.** Mirror jjx/vvx — `Command::new("git").args([...]).current_dir(&root).output()`. Stage with explicit file path (never `git add -A`); commit messages produced via Rust string formatting. No `--no-verify`, no `--amend`.

### Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

### Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section (5 human steps).

### Coupling to ₣A_

This heat runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces. ₣A_'s AAE+AAF+AAG sequence is the one-time cutover that informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence; pristine-tier construction proceeds in parallel and codifies the lessons. BBAAG's first end-to-end run is the natural validation point for both heats together.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap; AAE+AAF+AAG one-time burn-in informs but doesn't codify
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — RBLM Lifecycle Marshal; `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime, deletes all RBRAs, blanks hallmark/depot-scoped vessel fields. Marshal-zero signature is baked here per BBAAB.
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhocd_credential_director.sh` / `rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh` — handbook tracks describing director "build access" and retriever "pull/tally access" — operations the lifecycle cases 4 and 5 must verify
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
- Recent spook commits informing the bug class: kludge-aware-charge-prereq (₢BAAAH), ZRBOB_PROJECT extraction (f7146d1d), rbob_charged_predicate fix (a8eb7311), reliquary integrity-broken negative-test residue (recent ₣BA verification)
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_handbook.rs` — non-context tabtarget shell-out reference pattern for handbook-style cases (no fact files)
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdri_invocation.rs` — fact-file invocation pattern: `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact` for cases that need to capture identifying values
- `Tools/rbk/rbrr_cli.sh` — kindle architecture documented in Implementation patterns; sourcing-over-env behavior locked the throwaway mechanism choice

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAK

## Character

Sibling pace to BBAAB and BBAAJ. Implements the more complex half of pristine-lifecycle: retriever lifecycle (case 4) and director lifecycle (case 5). Each case creates a throwaway governor + the role SA, exercises the credential via access-probe, then forfeits.

Includes the assay/canonical file movement choreography: SA-creator writes RBRA to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`; test stages to `assay/`; test copies `assay/` → canonical role path for probe; test cleans canonical and assay before forfeit. Mirrors operator-flow file movements rather than extending the probe to accept arbitrary paths.

**Absorbs rbrr-completion fix-up.** BBAAJ landed cases 2-3 with `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` setting only `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` after levy. `zrbrr_enforce` (which runs on every non-`rbgp_depot_levy` invocation per `rbgp_cli.sh` differential furnish) requires `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` and `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` non-blank too — both blanked by marshal-zero. Credentialed cases 2-5 will fail at the first non-levy command without those fields populated. K lands two new `rbgp_fact_*` files for the values `rbgp_depot_levy` computes (`z_repository_name`, `z_pool_stem`), generalizes the install helper to write all three post-levy fields in one commit, and back-patches cases 2 and 3 to use it. Cases 4 and 5 follow the same pattern.

**BBAAJ landed at `e59c49b8`** — `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` and the throwaway-prefix consts are stable and `pub(crate)`. The thread-local invocation context is bridged by `rbtdrc_with_ctx` (now `pub(crate)` after BBAAJ); cases 4-5 use it the same way cases 2-3 do.

## Locked patterns

Implementation patterns are locked in the paddock under "Implementation patterns (locked during BBAAJ)". Read those before implementing. Reuse:

- `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(root)` — landed `pub(crate)` at e59c49b8; call as first step of cases 4 and 5 (idempotent — no-op if already invoked in same fixture run).
- `RBTDRP_THROWAWAY_CLOUD_PREFIX` / `RBTDRP_THROWAWAY_RUNTIME_PREFIX` — `pub(crate)` constants; reference by name rather than re-declaring `"prlc-"` / `"prlr-"` string literals.
- Field-const pattern — BBAAJ extracted `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_*` as private module-scope consts; mirror this pattern for any new RBRR field-name strings K introduces (see `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` below).
- Fact-file invocation pattern (BBAAJ-locked): tabtargets that emit identifying values write `rbgp_fact_*` files via `buf_write_fact`; cases consume via `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`.
- `rbtdrc_with_ctx` (now `pub(crate)` after BBAAJ) for thread-local context bridging — same shape as case 3.
- `rbtdri_find_tabtarget` (with role as imprint) for `rbtd-ap` — imprint channel.
- Git shell-out style: `rbtdrp_git_add_and_commit` (BBAAJ-factored helper) handles the `git add <file> && git commit -m <msg>` pair.

## Work

### Step 1 — rbrr-post-levy completion (foundational, lands first)

**Bash + spec edits (do BEFORE Rust changes):**

- `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh`: add two consts inside the existing readonly block (next to the `RBGP_FACT_*` pair from BBAAJ):
  ```
  readonly RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY="rbgp_fact_gar_repository"
  readonly RBGP_FACT_GCB_POOL_STEM="rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem"
  ```
- `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh::rbgp_depot_levy`: after `z_repository_name` is computed (search for the local declaration), `buf_write_fact "${RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY}" "${z_repository_name}"`. Same for `z_pool_stem` (search the body — assigned during pool-stem derivation around the worker-pool block). Place each `buf_write_fact` call immediately after the value becomes known so a partial-failure run still emits whatever was computed.
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`: extend the Payor Fact Files section (added in BBAAJ) with two new linked terms — `rbgp_fact_gar_repository` and `rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem`. Add their attribute references to the mapping section near the existing `rbgp_fact_*` entries. Apply CMK MCM linked-term shape (attribute reference + anchor + definition with `axpof_fact` voicing).

**Rust edits:**

- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrp_pristine.rs`:
  - Add `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` / `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` private consts (mirror the existing `RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`).
  - Add `RBTDRP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY` / `RBTDRP_FACT_GCB_POOL_STEM` consts mirroring the bash side.
  - **Rename** `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id(root, project_id)` → `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr(root, project_id, gar_repository, gcb_pool_stem)`. Single rbrr.env read → `rbtdrp_replace_env_fields` with three entries → single write → single commit. Commit message: `"pristine-lifecycle fixture: set RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID/RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY/RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM"`.
  - Patch `rbtdrp_depot_lifecycle_impl` (case 2): after the levy invocation, read all three facts; call `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr` BEFORE the `list-present` invocation (which runs enforce). Tee the three values to per-case files for diagnostics.
  - Patch `rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle_impl` (case 3): replace the existing `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` call with the new three-arg helper, sourcing all three values from the levy result's facts.

### Step 2 — Cases 4 and 5

#### Pre-conditions (per case)

- Each case pre-checks canonical role path empty (refuse with diagnostic if not — single-case independence requires clean canonical paths for the role being tested AND for governor, since each case mantles its own throwaway governor).

#### Case 4: retriever-lifecycle (one GCP project per run + light read probe)

- Call `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(root)` (idempotent).
- Pre-check: governor and retriever canonical RBRA paths empty.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dL", &["pristr01"], &[])` — levy own throwaway depot (distinct moniker from cases 2/3).
- Read all three post-levy facts (`rbgp_fact_depot_project_id`, `rbgp_fact_gar_repository`, `rbgp_fact_gcb_pool_stem`).
- Call `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr(root, project_id, gar_repository, gcb_pool_stem)`.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aM", &[], &[])` → mantle throwaway governor; capture governor SA email via `rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email` fact.
- Move: `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/$instance.rbra` → `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` → `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` (canonical).
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aC", &[], &[])` → charter throwaway retriever (its RBRA in `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`).
- Move: `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/$instance.rbra` → `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` (overwrite) → `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` (canonical).
- `rbtdri_invoke_imprint(ctx, "rbtd-ap", "retriever", &[])` → access-probe proves retriever permissions resolve.
- Cleanup: rm canonical retriever, rm canonical governor, rm assay.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aF", &[&retriever_email], &[])` → forfeit retriever.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-aF", &[&governor_email], &[])` → forfeit governor.
- `rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, "rbw-dU", &[&project_id], &[("BURE_CONFIRM", "skip")])` → unmake own depot.

#### Case 5: director-lifecycle (one GCP project per run + light Cloud Build / GAR write probe)

Same shape as case 4 but with `rbw-aK` (knight director) and `rbtd-ap director`. Moniker: `pristd01`.

#### Open question for implementation time (carried from prior docket revs)

`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` / `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE` are bash-side derived constants from `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`. The Rust fixture either re-derives the paths from RBRR fields or queries them by sourcing rbdc — pick at implementation time. Re-derivation is simpler if the path shape is stable (e.g., `${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/<role>/rbra.env` plus assay equivalent).

Similarly: cases 4/5 need to capture the role-SA email + RBRA file location after charter/knight. Whether `rbgg_charter_retriever` / `rbgg_knight_director` already emit fact files, or need new ones (`rbgp_fact_retriever_sa_email` / `rbgp_fact_director_sa_email`), or whether the email is discoverable via the BURD_OUTPUT_DIR file naming — choose at implementation time. Prefer fact files for symmetry with the governor pattern (BBAAJ).

### Step 3 — Section + manifest registration

- Add section `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` to `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` (after `pristine-lifecycle-simple` from BBAAJ). Final layout: §1 gate → §2 simple → §3 credentialed.
- Add colophon consts to `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`: `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_CHARTER` (rbw-aC), `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_KNIGHT` (rbw-aK).
- `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ACCESS_PROBE` (rbtd-ap) is already in `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE` — re-declare for `pristine-lifecycle` for clarity.
- Extend the `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` required-colophon array (currently 6 entries from BBAAJ) with the three new entries.

## Verification

- Build green: `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh`.
- Unit tests green: `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh`. The renamed helper invalidates the BBAAJ-landed `rbtdtp_pristine.rs` test surface; if any test references `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` by name, update or replace.
- Fast suite green: `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`.
- Single-case `depot-lifecycle` (post-rename): list/unmake commands now succeed past `zrbrr_enforce` because all three RBRR fields are populated. Operator-action.
- Single-case `governor-lifecycle`: same, plus governor lifecycle. Operator-action.
- Single-case `retriever-lifecycle`: creates throwaway depot+gov+ret, probe passes, cleans up cleanly; refuses with diagnostic if canonical role paths occupied. Operator-action.
- Single-case `director-lifecycle`: same for director. Operator-action.
- Full fixture on marshal-zero state: cases 1-5 all pass with one rbrr.env install commit (prefixes), then four post-levy commits (one per case 2/3/4/5), each documenting the depot they're running against.

## Out of scope

- Cases 1-3 design (BBAAB and BBAAJ); cases 2/3 are only patched here to consume the new helper, not redesigned.
- Canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC).
- Crash-recovery cleanup of orphan SAs and orphan throwaway depots — handled via soft-delete graveyard discipline (depot) and operator-driven `rbw-aF` (SAs); a partial-failure run accumulates a soft-delete-pending project, which is acceptable per the "start-over-from-zero" failure-mode contract.

## Quota footprint (full fixture run)

Four throwaway depots per pristine-lifecycle run (one per case 2, 3, 4, 5). Each goes to `DELETE_REQUESTED` on case cleanup; soft-delete graveyard accepted. Plus one canonical depot from BBAAC for the rest of `rbw-tP`. Five quota-bearing project creations per pristine release-qualification cycle.

content.text:
Mounted ₣BB rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification. Pace ₢BBAAK is **phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-credentialed** — sibling to BBAAJ (which landed at `e59c49b8`).

**Heat shape (paddock):** Construct `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — third release tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero was just committed. Pristine-lifecycle fixture has 5 cases: §1 gate (BBAAB ✅), §2 simple cases 2-3 depot/governor (BBAAJ ✅), §3 credentialed cases 4-5 retriever/director (this pace).

**Recent work:**
- BBAAJ (e59c49b8): Cases 2-3 with fact-file invocation pattern; locked `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` + `rbtdrc_with_ctx` as `pub(crate)`. Surfaced concern: `zrbrr_enforce` requires `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY` and `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` non-blank, both blanked by marshal-zero. Cases 4-5 will fail without them.

**Silks assessment:** `phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-credentialed` fits the docket — credentialed = real SA cred + access-probe. No rename needed.

**Approach (3 sequential steps):**
1. **rbrr-post-levy completion (foundational, lands first)** — bash: add `RBGP_FACT_GAR_REPOSITORY`/`RBGP_FACT_GCB_POOL_STEM` consts in RBGC, emit via `buf_write_fact` in `rbgp_depot_levy`; spec: extend RBS0 Payor Fact Files section with two new linked terms; Rust: rename `rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id` → `rbtdrp_install_post_levy_rbrr` (3 fields, 1 commit), back-patch cases 2 and 3.
2. **Cases 4 + 5** — case 4 retriever (`rbw-aC` + `rbtd-ap retriever`), case 5 director (`rbw-aK` + `rbtd-ap director`); same shape: throwaway prefixes → levy own depot → install post-levy rbrr → mantle throwaway governor → assay/canonical file movement → charter/knight role SA → assay/canonical → probe → cleanup → forfeits → unmake. Open Q's at implementation: RBDC path re-derivation vs. sourcing; whether `rbgg_charter_retriever`/`rbgg_knight_director` need new fact files for SA email (prefer yes for symmetry).
3. **Section + manifest registration** — add `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` to `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`; add `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_CHARTER`/`GOV_KNIGHT`; extend fixture-required-colophon array.

**Tradeoff to flag:** Step 1's helper rename is mechanical but invalidates BBAAJ's fresh test surface in `rbtdtp_pristine.rs`. Carries a small "did we destabilize what just landed?" risk — mitigated by running fast suite immediately after Step 1.

**Execution strategy:** Sequential, opus tier. Architectural judgment needed at the two open questions (Step 2 implementation time). Step 1 should land as a discrete notch — it stands alone as a valuable fix-up even if Step 2 takes longer. Step 2 can land as one or two notches (case 4 then case 5, or both together depending on test surface clarity).

Verification cadence:
- After Step 1: `rbtd-b`, `rbtd-t`, `rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` — must stay green
- After Step 2/3: same + manual operator runs of `rbtd-s.SingleCase.pristine-lifecycle.sh retriever-lifecycle` / `director-lifecycle` (these need GCP credentials + quota)

Ready to proceed with Step 1 (rbrr-post-levy completion)?

# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)

zrbgc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"

  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX=10

  # Global resource validation patterns
  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS
  # Depot:  rbwg-d-[name]-YYMMDDHHMMSS
  # Bucket: rbwg-b-[name]-YYMMDDHHMMSS
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-[a-z0-9-]+-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_BUCKET_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET}-[a-z0-9-]+-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"

  # Basic Configuration
  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"
  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_ROLE="rbw-payor"
  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_APP_NAME="Recipe Bottle Payor"

  # Service Account Prefixes
  readonly RBGC_MASON_PREFIX="mason"

  # Timeouts
  readonly RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC=90
  readonly RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC=3
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX=7
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC=10

  # URL Roots & Well-known Endpoints
  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL="https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM="https://iam.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM="https://cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING="https://cloudbilling.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE="https://storage.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SECRETMANAGER="https://secretmanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_CONSOLE_URL="https://console.cloud.google.com/"
  readonly RBGC_SIGNUP_URL="https://cloud.google.com/free"

  # OAuth Scopes
  readonly RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM="https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"

  # Service Usage Service Identifiers
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_IAM="iam.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_CRM="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="artifactregistry.googleapis.com"

  # Email/Domain Assembly
  readonly RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN="iam.gserviceaccount.com"

  # API Version Paths
  readonly RBGC_IAM_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_V3="/v3"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1="/v1beta1"
  readonly RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1="/storage/v1"
  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_UPLOAD="/upload/storage/v1"
  readonly RBGC_SECRETMANAGER_V1="/v1"

  # REST Path Fragments
  readonly RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS="/projects"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_LOCATIONS="/locations"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_REPOSITORIES="/repositories"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS="/serviceAccounts"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_KEYS="/keys"

  # REST Operation Suffixes
  readonly RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":getIamPolicy"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":setIamPolicy"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX=":enable"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_PATH_SERVICES="/services"

  # Operation Prefixes
  readonly RBGC_OP_PREFIX_GLOBAL="operations/"

  # Ark Artifact Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Each ark type is a plain basename sibling under <prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/.
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE="image"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT="about"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH="vouch"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS="diags"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST="attest"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH="pouch"

  # GAR Categorical Namespaces (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Top-level prefix-rooted namespaces under which hallmark/reliquary/enshrine
  # arks are stored as plain basename siblings. Consumed by rbgl_GarLayout.sh.
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="hallmarks"
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="reliquaries"
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES="enshrines"

  # Reliquary Tool Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Canonical tool names under <prefix>reliquaries/<date>/. Authoritative
  # manifest lives in rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh.
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD="gcloud"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER="docker"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE="alpine"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT="syft"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT="binfmt"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO="skopeo"

  # Fact-file filenames (written to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by producers, read by tests)
  readonly RBF_FACT_HALLMARK="rbf_fact_hallmark"
  readonly RBF_FACT_BUILD_ID="rbf_fact_build_id"
  readonly RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT="rbf_fact_gar_root"
  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM="rbf_fact_ark_stem"
  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_YIELD="rbf_fact_ark_yield"
  readonly RBF_FACT_RELIQUARY="rbf_fact_reliquary"

  # Payor fact-file filenames (depot/governor identifying values)
  readonly RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID="rbgp_fact_depot_project_id"
  readonly RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL="rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email"

  # Artifact Registry (GAR) Composition
  readonly RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX="-docker.pkg.dev"


  # Canonical Role IDs
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER="roles/artifactregistry.reader"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_WRITER="roles/artifactregistry.writer"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_ADMIN="roles/artifactregistry.admin"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER="roles/containeranalysis.occurrences.viewer"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR="roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor"

  # Common API Base Paths (project-independent)
  readonly RBGC_API_BASE_GCS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}"

  # Cloud Resource Manager - Liens API
  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_LIST_LIENS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"
  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_DELETE_LIEN="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"

  # Google Cloud Storage (GCS) APIs (project-independent)
  readonly RBGC_API_GCS_BUCKETS="${RBGC_API_BASE_GCS}/b"

  readonly RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM="linux/amd64"


  # Worker pool infrastructure (dual pools: tether + airgap)
  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER="-tether"
  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP="-airgap"
  readonly RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX="-pool"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS="/workerPools"

  readonly ZRBGC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgc not kindled - call zrbgc_kindle first"
}
